title: Scenario 07 External Authority Bridge Boundary Delta Record
bridge_question: Does a Fork-preserved internal record establish external authority conclusions?

boundary_sequence.claim_state_after_transition: The internal record remains inspectable for reconstruction and boundary-state review; no external admissibility, compliance, approval, legal sufficiency, acceptance, or authority transfers.
An external actor attempts to treat inspectability as an authority-bearing conclusion; the record does not support that inference.

supported_after_transition: The external recipient can inspect the preserved internal record.
The record can show what was claimed and not claimed internally.
The record can show that external authority conclusions require separate support.
fork_role: Fork records the external authority bridge state without transferring authority, establishing admissibility, determining compliance, approving the decision, or judging correctness.
limitations: This BDR records inspectability and unsupported expansion only.
This BDR does not establish any external authority conclusion.
External authority conclusions require separate authority, rule, standard, or decision process.
